summary: End-to-end frontend testing of Shannon Engineering site (frontend-only build). All requested flows verified: Home (hero video, CTAs), Navbar routing + mobile menu, About page, Services (6 cards), Projects (9 cards + filters: Commercial=3, Residential=3, Infrastructure=3), Project Detail (info strip, valid slug load), invalid slug -> redirect to /projects, Contact form (floating-label inputs, mocked submit -> sonner success toast 'Inquiry received...', form clears on success), Map iframe, Footer rendering. Zero browser console errors observed during the run. Page transitions and framer-motion animations render cleanly.
critical_code_review_comments: Contact form submission is intentionally MOCKED (frontend setTimeout 900ms + sonner toast). No backend persistence — confirmed by user choice in problem statement.
Filters sticky bar uses top-[68px] which works at the current navbar height; if navbar padding changes, may need to be variable-driven.
Hero video has poster fallback and playsInline — good. Consider adding a prefers-reduced-motion check for accessibility (non-blocking).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Site is frontend-only with mocked contact submission. data-testid coverage is excellent (hero-view-projects, hero-contact-us, nav-link-{label}, nav-mobile-toggle, nav-mobile-panel, filter-{cat}, project-card-{slug}, contact-input-{name}, contact-submit, map-embed, home-page, projects-page, contact-page). When testing the contact submit, do NOT use force=True on click — it can bypass native form submission. Use a plain page.click. Project slugs from data: skyline-tower (Commercial). Invalid project slugs correctly redirect to /projects.
